title: Lesson 39
paragraph: This lesson focuses on practical B1 vocabulary for everyday communication.

words.meaning: to give an idea or plan for someone to think about
words.example: I suggest taking the earlier train.

words.meaning: to notice flavor with your mouth
words.example: Taste the soup before you add salt.

words.meaning: to tell someone you are grateful
words.example: Remember to thank your host after dinner.

words.meaning: to send something through the air with your hand
words.example: Please throw the ball to me.

words.meaning: to fasten something with string or a similar object
words.example: Tie your shoes before you run.

words.meaning: to put your hand or finger on something
words.example: Do not touch the hot pan.

words.meaning: to teach or practice a skill
words.example: The company will train new workers.

words.meaning: to go and spend time in a place or with someone
words.example: We visit our grandparents every month.

words.meaning: to choose someone or something in an election or group decision
words.example: Students vote for the class leader.
